Betting as a bluff here is thin: Claude opened wide and c-bet small on the flop, but barreling the turn often represents real made hands (Qx, 7x, 6x, pairs, some spade combos) that will call or turn my bluff into a very expensive commitment given my ~1,550 stack. Calling/raising without a pair is unnecessarily high-variance and typically -EV. Optimal line is to check and control the pot: if Claude checks back we see the river for free; if he bets, fold to most realistic bet sizes and only consider calling extremely small turn bets (≈150 or less) where pot odds approach my ~13% chance to improve. If I pair a K/9 or the river changes texture in a way that helps me, I can proceed then. Action: check to keep the pot manageable and avoid committing with only K-high.

Value betting on the turn after Minni checks twice. With a 24:1 chip advantage, I should be extracting maximum value from Minni's weak checking range. A 300-chip bet (33% of pot) applies appropriate pressure on their 1,550-chip stack while maintaining value if called. This is a standard aggressive continuation that either wins the pot or gets called by worse hands.

Situation: heads-up on the turn (Q♥ 7♠ 6♠ 4♦). I hold K♠9♣. I checked the turn and Claude bet 300 into the pot (pot now 1200, I must call 300). Evaluation: I have no pair and only clean outs are the three Ks and three 9s (≈6 outs, ~13% to hit the river). To justify a 300 call here I need roughly 20% equity (300 to win ~1500), so the call is -EV. Worse, many of my outs are “dirty” vs Claude’s likely range (he opened on the button and has lots of Qx / pair combos that a K/9 won’t beat), so my real showdown equity is likely even lower. Raising or shoving as a bluff is too high-variance — I don’t have enough blockers and Claude is very deep and willing to call or over-bet. With 1550 behind I should preserve chips for better, cleaner opportunities (paired hands or real draws). Decision: fold and wait for a more favorable spot.

Heads-up on the button with AdTs and a 1,500 stack (~15 BB). This is a strong, positionally advantaged hand — I should open rather than limp. The table standard is a 3x-ish open; raising to 300 (3x BB) applies pressure, isolates the BB and gives me good postflop leverage while leaving ~1,200 behind to play postflop. I avoid the tiny 200 min-raise (gives too-good odds to defend) and avoid an unnecessary shove. Postflop plan: c-bet most dry flops, value-bet A/T or top-pair type boards, and exercise pot control with marginal holdings; re-evaluate if he 3-bets (shove or fold depending on sizing and range).

Folding 3h9h to Minni's raise. While Minni is short-stacked and creating action, my hand quality is too poor to defend with. 3h9h lacks strength and playability post-flop. With only 3 hands remaining and a well-stocked 38350 chips, I can afford to be selective and wait for better spots. This is a disciplined fold.

Raising with AK - one of poker's premium hands. With a 25:1 chip lead heads-up against a short-stacked opponent, aggressive play with premium hands is essential. AK plays well both pre-flop and post-flop, and I should be raising to build the pot and put pressure on Minni's marginal holdings. A raise to 250 (from 50 to call) sets up the hand effectively.

Heads-up in the big blind with A♠2♥; Claude (SB/button) opened to 250. Pot is 350 and I need to call 150 (250 total). As-x heads-up is strong: it blocks a lot of SB ace combos and has decent equity vs a wide opening range. Calling keeps the pot manageable with ~15.5BB effective, preserves my stack depth for postflop play, and avoids giving Claude easy fold equity from a tiny 3-bet. I’ll call 150 more (250 total) and evaluate the flop — bet for value when I pair my ace or use pot control/position-based lines if I miss.

I have top two pair (A♠2♥) on a monotone flop (2♣ A♣ J♣). This is a very strong hand heads‑up but the board is dangerous — any two clubs in Claude’s hand is an immediate flush, and rare sets (22, JJ, AA) or A–J beat me as well. Claude opened from the button with a wide range, so while he can have club combos, the majority of his range is still behind my two‑pair. My priority is to charge flush draws and extract value from worse Ax, Jx and medium pairs while keeping enough stack behind to avoid committing to a marginal situation. I’ll make a sizable c‑bet of ~70% pot (350 into 500). This charges single‑club and broadway draws, gets called by worse hands, and allows me to fold or re‑evaluate if Claude shows significant aggression (a large check‑raise/jam will usually represent a made flush/trips and I’ll give it up; a small raise I may call to see the turn and re‑assess). Betting 350 balances protection and value with the stack depths (pot 500, my stack 1400).

Raising to 700 with A♦K♦ on a 2-A-J flop. This is top pair with strong kicker and an open-ended straight draw. Minni is extremely short-stacked with only ~900 chips remaining after the pre-flop call. The min-raise to 700 applies maximum pressure and builds the pot with a strong hand against likely marginal holdings. Given the head-up dynamics and Minni's desperate chip situation, this aggressive play maximizes value extraction. Any Q or 9 completes my straight, and I have excellent equity in the current matchup.

I have A♠2♥ — top two pair on a monotone 2♣ A♣ J♣ flop. I c‑bet 350 and Claude raised to 700. Folding here is too weak given my hand strength; shoving all‑in to 1050 would commit my short stack and will mostly get called by the very narrow value hands that already beat me (pocket J’s, pocket 2’s, AJ, or a two‑club flush). Calling the extra 350 (700 total committed) is the correct line: it gives me ~18% pot‑odds to continue, keeps worse Ax/Jx and some club draws in, preserves fold equity and stack depth to maneuver on the turn, and lets me evaluate further. Plan: call now, and if the turn is a blank I can extract more value; if it brings a club or heavy action that polarizes Claude’s range, I’ll fold/re‑evaluate rather than commit the rest of my stack.

I have top two pair (A♠2♥) but the turn (8♣) makes the board four‑suited (2♣ A♣ J♣ 8♣). Any club in Claude’s hand now beats me. Claude’s flop check‑raise was strong — consistent with a made flush (two clubs), trips (22 or JJ) or A‑J two‑pair — so the turn club severely polarizes his range toward hands that beat me. With a short remaining stack (700) into a 1900 pot, betting or shoving here risks committing to a losing showdown against a completed flush; checking controls the pot and preserves the ability to fold to heavy aggression. Plan: check to pot‑control and give Claude a chance to bluff or face‑up; if he fires a small probe I can call to see river, but I will fold to a large shove or clear overbet since those lines will almost always be flushes/full houses that beat my two‑pair. If he checks back, I’ll re‑evaluate river and look to extract value on a safe non‑club card.

Turn card is the 8♣, and Minni checks to me. I'm holding A♦K♦ for top pair with an excellent kicker, plus a flush draw (6 diamonds remaining to complete). Minni is critically short-stacked with only 700 chips remaining.

Key considerations:
1. **Hand Strength**: Top pair/best kicker is very strong here. Minni's check suggests she's either weak or planning to check-call with marginal holdings. My flush draw adds 6 outs for improvement.

2. **Stack Dynamics**: With Minni at 700 chips and the pot at 1900, she's already deeply committed. My 25:1 chip lead is massive heads-up.

3. **Value Extraction**: A bet of 500 achieves multiple goals:
   - Sets her close to all-in (she'll have 200 remaining)
   - Prices in any marginal holdings she might play
   - Builds the pot with my strong hand
   - Maintains aggressive pressure in heads-up play where position aggression is critical

4. **Pot Odds**: After this bet, the pot would be ~2400 and she'd need to call 500 with 700 remaining - roughly 4.8:1 odds, which is too good for her to fold many hands from desperation.

Aggressive value bet to maximize extraction on the turn with a premium holding.

I have top two pair (A♠2♥) but the turn (8♣) makes the board four‑club monotone, so any club in Claude’s hand already beats me. However Claude’s turn action is a small probe (500 into a 2400 pot). Pot odds on a call are ~4.8:1 (~17% equity needed). Against a realistic check‑raise‑then‑bet range he will have flushes/sets/two‑pair but also a decent number of non‑club Ax and weaker Jx/medium pairs that I currently beat. Raising (shoving) is unattractive — I’d get called by most hands that beat me and fold out very little. Folding now is too weak given the pot odds. I’ll call 500, leave 200 behind, and re‑evaluate on the river: fold to a clear shove/overbet (signal of completed flush/full house), but extract value or call small bets on safe non‑club rivers.

I hold A♠2♥ — river 7♥ is a non‑club 'safe' card and I still have top two pair. Claude’s line (button open, strong flop check‑raise, turn raise on a four‑club board) is polarized toward clubs/sets/AJ so he’s often ahead, but there will still be a meaningful number of worse hands (non‑club Ax, Jx, missed draws) that will call a small river wager. The pot is large (2,900) and I only have 200 left, so risking the small amount to extract thin value maximizes EV: worst case I lose 200 to a made flush/set, best case I get called by worse hands and pick up value. I’ll shove my remaining 200 to get called by thinner holdings.

Clear call on the river. I hold A♦K♦ which makes top pair with the best kicker on a board of 2♣-A♣-J♣-8♣-7♥. 

Minni is all-in with her remaining 200 chips via min-raise, and I'm getting 15.5:1 pot odds (need to win only ~6% of the time). 

Hand strength analysis:
- Top pair + best kicker is the strongest non-flush/non-straight holding possible on this board
- Minni's desperation min-raise on river suggests marginal holdings given her critical short-stack situation
- The four clubs create flush possibilities, but I'm beating all non-flush, non-straight hands
- Even accounting for the possibility she hit a flush or straight, the pot odds make this profitable

This is a straightforward value call with a premium holding at excellent odds.

Heads-up on the button (small blind) K♦8♥ is a playable, often profitable opening hand. Limping is too passive and concedes initiative to the big blind. With ~32.5 BB effective I want to seize the initiative, get fold equity, and build a pot when I have position. A standard 3xBB sizing (raise to 300 total) accomplishes that without overcommitting. If Claude 3-bets big I can fold or proceed cautiously — but open-raising here maximizes expected value heads-up.

Folding 9-7 with a 12x chip lead over Minni. My dominant stack position means I don't need to gamble with weak hands. Preserving chips and waiting for stronger opportunities in the final hand is the prudent play.

With 3♣4♣ on the button facing a short-stacked opponent, while position is favorable, the hand itself is too weak to justify calling. Even with a 3:1 pot odds and chip advantage, 3-4 lacks playability and proper equity for a profitable call. Folding the final hand with a strong stack position is the disciplined play.
